text:
French pole vaulter Anthony Ammirati has spoken out after going viral for the unfortunate end to hisOlympics.

Ammirati was hoping to bag a medal in front of his home crowd at the Stade de France but was left disappointed after his penis caused his downfall.

The 21-year-old, who won gold in this event at the World Athletics U20 Championships in 2022, looked to have the height to clear 5.70m and take a big step towards the pole vault finalwhen his manhood got in the way.

The unfortunate incident effectively ended Ammirati’s Olympic hopes, with the Frenchman eventually finishing 12th in Group A with a height of 5.60m.

5.70m was enough for several athletes to advance to the final but Ammirati was denied a place in the showpiece event and admitted he was ‘a bit gutted’ to miss out.

‘It’s a big disappointment,’ Ammirati said. ‘I’m a bit gutted, because I didn’t miss anything on the third attempt at 5.70m.

‘What I did miss was a bit of jumping in training to fine-tune the settings, just a technical session.

Anthony Ammirati failed the bar and the commentators are clearly having a hard time acknowledging what happened ð HELP I’M DYINGpic.twitter.com/5hOHttVA5g

‘I was 100 percent physically, but I was missing a bit of pole vault. The conditions were good. It’s the first time I’ve started a competition without any stress.

‘As I was a total outsider, I only had one goal: to interact with the audience. I was almost there.’

ENVOL, a pole vault club based in France, sent a message to Ammirati following his Olympics disappointment.

‘A fifteenth place in qualifying for the Paris Olympics with 5m60,’ they posted on Instagram.

‘We are proud of you, even if we are frustrated like you for this bar at 5m70. We wish you an unforgettable Olympic experience.’

Fellow French pole vaulter Thibaut Collet also failed to reach the final and said he was ‘ashamed’ of his performance.

‘I am extremely ashamed of my performance,’ he said. ‘I don’t understand: the last few weeks of preparation were too good, we worked really hard.

‘The hardest thing is to tell yourself that there is nothing I could have done better.’

France’s Robin Emig, who suffered the same fate, added: ‘I can only blame myself.

‘This competition is like my summer season: I’m struggling to find the sensations and to make it happen.’

title: Pole vaulter breaks silence after his penis costs him chance of Olympic medal | Metro News
description: French pole vaulter Anthony Ammirati has spoken out after going viral for the unfortunate end to his Olympics.
